system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss upwind;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error is the same as before but now occurring in `0/p_rgh`:

```
Cannot find patchField entry for topWall
```

This means the `0/p_rgh` file is still referencing patches that don't exist in the mesh. Looking at the current `0/p_rgh` file, I can see it references `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall`. However, looking carefully at the actual file content provided, the `0/p_rgh` file actually **already has been fixed** in this attempt — it correctly lists `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, and `backWall`.

Wait — let me re-examine the current files more carefully. The `0/p_rgh` file currently shows:

```
boundaryField
{
    upperWall
    {
        ...
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        ...
    }
    ...
}
```

But the `blockMeshDict` defines `topWall` and `bottomWall`, not `upperWall` and `lowerWall`. So even though I suggested fixing it last time, the file appears to still have the old incorrect patch names!

Similarly, looking at all the `0/` files (T, U, alphat, epsilon, k, nut, p), they all still reference patches that don't exist in the mesh (`upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `frontAndBack`), OR in some cases they've been partially fixed but others remain broken.

Let me check what the actual current state is. The key issue across ALL `0/` files is that they reference patches that don't exist in `blockMeshDict`. Every single one needs to be checked and corrected.

## Proposed Fixes

### 1. Fix `0/p_rgh` — Replace `upperWall`→`topWall`, `lowerWall`→`bottomWall`, remove `frontAndBack`

The `0/p_rgh` file currently has `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`? Actually looking at the provided content, it shows `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall` — but `upperWall`/`lowerWall` don't exist, and the values are wrong too. It should have `topWall` and `bottomWall` with correct types.

Actually wait — the provided `0/p_rgh` shows `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. This is wrong. All instances of `upperWall` must become `topWall`, and all instances of `lowerWall` must become `bottomWall`. There should also be no `frontAndBack`.

### 2. Fix `0/T` — Same issue: rename `upperWall`→`topWall`, `lowerWall`→`bottomWall`, remove `frontAndBack`

### 3. Fix `0/U` — Same issue: rename `floor`, `ceiling`, `fixedWalls` entries to the correct six patch names

### 4. Fix `0/alphat` — Same issue: rename entries to match the six patches

### 5. Fix `0/epsilon` — Same issue

### 6. Fix `0/k` — Same issue

### 7. Fix `0/nut` — Same issue

Every single `0/*.` file must have its boundary field entries renamed so they exactly match the six patch names from `blockMeshDict`: `topWall`, `bottomWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`, `frontWall`, `backWall`. No exceptions. No `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `frontAndBack`, `floor`, `ceiling`, or `fixedWalls` entries.

The root cause across all attempts is that the boundary condition files were copied/modified but never fully corrected — some patches got renamed while others remained broken. Every file in the `0/` directory needs complete verification and correction.</reviewer_analysis>

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t=1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 301 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
